message:
fix(responsiveness + tests): align disabled-path telemetry with reported capacity + RAII env-guard for governor tests

responsiveness.rs
  telemetry_snapshot() previously reported
  current_capacity_pct = self.current_capacity.load(Relaxed) even when
  CASS_RESPONSIVENESS_DISABLE / self.cfg.disabled was in effect. But
  every caller that actually consumes the governor scalar
  (`effective_worker_count`, `current_capacity_pct`) short-circuits to
  100 in that case, so robot-mode consumers were seeing two different
  "current" numbers for the same process: the telemetry atomic could
  hold a stale shrunken value from a pre-disable sampler tick while
  the fan-out path pretended the governor was at 100. Fix: compute
  `disabled` once and, when disabled, report 100 for
  current_capacity_pct (and reuse the same bool for disabled_via_env).
  No behavioral change to the fan-out path.

tests/search_latency_under_indexing.rs
  The governor-disabled regression test was setting
  CASS_RESPONSIVENESS_DISABLE=1 via a bare `unsafe std::env::set_var`
  and removing it at the bottom of the function. If the assertion
  panicked between those two calls, the env var leaked into any
  other test in the same binary that happened to run afterwards,
  giving spurious "pressured p95 was actually idle" results.
  Replace with a small RAII EnvGuard that captures the prior value
  in set() and restores it (or removes it) on Drop even when the
  body panics. Concentrates the `unsafe` into one reviewable place
  and makes the test obviously cleanup-safe.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
